criteria.name: startup_ritual_clean_tree_at_commit
evidence: git status --short --branch returned only '## stsyg-glowing-waffle...origin/stsyg-glowing-waffle' (clean working tree).
git --no-pager log --oneline -10 top entry: '219760b feat(worker): add worker + scheduler with PostgreSQL job queue (F002 slice 2)' over '6049e7f F002 slice 1 ...'.
git rev-parse HEAD returned 219760b8bd6c2cbcce73c314a3f3bd34b200ed9d, matching the implementation commit under test.

Read agent-state/current_contract.json (F002 slice-2, required_evaluation_level 2, approved), feature_list.json (F002 passes=false), progress.md tail (Builder slice-2 entry), docker-compose.yml, migrations/versions/0002_worker_queue.py, and all apps/worker sources before grading.

criteria.name: service_topology_healthchecks_and_ordering
evidence: docker compose config --services returned exactly: postgres, api, scheduler, worker.

docker-compose.yml worker service: command python -m worker.main; healthcheck test [CMD, python, -m, worker.health, --service, worker] (interval 10s, timeout 10s, retries 5, start_period 30s); depends_on postgres condition: service_healthy.

docker-compose.yml scheduler service: command python -m worker.scheduler; healthcheck test [CMD, python, -m, worker.health, --service, scheduler] (interval 10s, retries 5, start_period 30s); depends_on postgres condition: service_healthy.
The web (React) frontend remains only in a commented '--- Deferred to a later F002 slice ---' block; no apps/web service is defined.

criteria.name: static_scope_review_no_frontend_domain_or_new_deps
evidence: git --no-pager diff main...HEAD --stat: 23 files changed, 1098 insertions(+), 78 deletions(-), limited to .env.example, agent-state contract/progress, apps/worker/** (new), docker-compose.yml, migrations/versions/0002_worker_queue.py, pyproject.toml, scripts/stack-smoke.{ps1,sh}, tests/unit/test_requirements_sync.py, tests/unit/test_worker_queue.py.

Directory probe: apps\web exists=False, apps\scheduler exists=False (scheduler runs from the shared apps/worker image), domain exists=False, catalogue exists=False, apps\api\app\domain exists=False. No catalogue/offer/evidence/region/quota/adapter code present.

apps/worker/requirements.txt pins (sqlalchemy==2.0.36, psycopg[binary]==3.2.3, pydantic-settings==2.7.0) are a strict subset of pyproject.toml [project.dependencies]; no new runtime framework, message broker, or managed-cloud dependency added. The queue is plain SQL (FOR UPDATE SKIP LOCKED / ON CONFLICT upsert).

Diff scan for '\bfree\b|\bZ0\b|zero-cost|$0' found only scoping/out-of-scope statements (e.g. 'no ... free-tier/Z0 claim is introduced', 'Z0 classification remain out of scope'); no unsupported service-is-free or Z0 claim was introduced.

criteria.name: regression_check_and_test_suites_green
evidence: pwsh -NoProfile -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File scripts\check.ps1 -NodeAudit exited 0 and ended ALL CHECKS PASSED with PASS for Ruff lint, Ruff format check (21 files), Pytest (42 passed, 2 skipped), Prettier check, ESLint, Secret scan, Python dependency audit (no known vulnerabilities), and Node dependency audit (0 vulnerabilities).

pwsh -NoProfile -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File scripts\test.ps1 exited 0: collected 44 items, 42 passed, 2 skipped (both the ATLAS_STACK_BASE_URL-gated integration tests), TESTS PASSED; tests/unit/test_worker_queue.py contributed 16 passing tests and test_requirements_sync.py 2 (including test_worker_pins_subset_of_declared).
The 2 skipped tests are the integration tests that require a live stack base URL (skipped in the offline suite), not failures.

criteria.name: migration_0002_creates_and_reverses_tables
evidence: From a fresh volume (stack-down.ps1 -Volumes removed stsyg-glowing-waffle_atlas_pgdata, then stack-up.ps1 built images and started the stack), docker compose exec api alembic current returned 0002_worker_queue (head).
smoke check 'Worker migration applied (job_queue + service_heartbeat)' PASS; independent to_regclass returned job_queue and service_heartbeat present after fresh up.

docker compose exec api alembic downgrade -1 ran '0002_worker_queue -> 0001_scaffold_baseline'; afterwards SELECT to_regclass('public.job_queue'), to_regclass('public.service_heartbeat') returned '|' (both NULL).

docker compose exec api alembic upgrade head ran '0001_scaffold_baseline -> 0002_worker_queue'; afterwards to_regclass returned 'job_queue|service_heartbeat' and alembic current returned 0002_worker_queue (head).

criteria.name: live_smoke_queue_processed_and_heartbeats_fresh
evidence: pwsh -NoProfile -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File scripts\stack-smoke.ps1 exited 0 with STACK SMOKE PASSED across all 8 checks (API liveness, API readiness, migration applied, worker migration applied, worker healthy, scheduler healthy, queue processed >=1 done, heartbeats fresh).
Independent psql: SELECT status, count(*) FROM job_queue GROUP BY status returned 'done | 9' (>=1 job reached done).

Independent psql: SELECT service, count(*), max(last_beat_at) FROM service_heartbeat GROUP BY service returned worker (1 row, 2026-07-16 21:18:32+00) and scheduler (1 row, 2026-07-16 21:18:30+00) — one fresh row per service.

criteria.name: container_health_all_four_healthy
evidence: docker compose ps reported all four containers Up and (healthy): stsyg-glowing-waffle-api-1, -postgres-1, -scheduler-1, -worker-1.
docker inspect -f '{{.State.Health.Status}}' for each service returned worker=healthy, scheduler=healthy, api=healthy, postgres=healthy.

criteria.name: negative_fail_closed_and_no_credential_leak
evidence: docker compose stop postgres; docker compose exec -T worker python -m worker.health --service worker printed 'database unreachable (OperationalError)' and exited 1; --service scheduler printed the same and exited 1.

Neither health message contained a connection string, the atlas password, 'postgres', or 'postgresql+psycopg://' — only the exception type name is surfaced (confirmed by inspecting the emitted lines).
After ~60s the containers flipped to unhealthy: docker inspect showed worker=unhealthy streak=5 and scheduler=unhealthy streak=5 (interval 10s x 5 retries), matching the configured healthcheck.
The worker's .State.Health.Log output lines were all 'database unreachable (OperationalError)' with no credential/connection-string leak.

Before restart worker and scheduler had RestartCount=0. docker compose start postgres; after ~45s both returned to healthy with RestartCount still 0 and unchanged StartedAt (2026-07-16T21:17:47Z) — the loops survived the outage and the containers did NOT restart.

criteria.name: restart_persistence_and_no_duplicate_heartbeats
evidence: pwsh scripts\stack-down.ps1 (without -Volumes, keeping the volume) then stack-up.ps1 then stack-smoke.ps1 exited 0 with STACK SMOKE PASSED.

After restart, SELECT service, count(*) FROM service_heartbeat GROUP BY service returned worker=1 and scheduler=1 — the ON CONFLICT (service) DO UPDATE upsert produced no duplicate rows across the restart.
SELECT count(*) FROM app_meta WHERE key='scaffold_initialized' AND value='true' returned 1, confirming the database and migrations survived the restart intact.

criteria.name: cleanup_stack_left_down
evidence: Final pwsh -NoProfile -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File scripts\stack-down.ps1 stopped and removed all four containers and the default network and ended STACK DOWN.
docker compose ps afterwards listed no running services (header only).
git status --short --branch remained clean ('## stsyg-glowing-waffle...origin/stsyg-glowing-waffle') — no product source was modified during evaluation.

commands_run: directory existence probe for apps/web, apps/scheduler, domain, catalogue, apps/api/app/domain
docker compose exec -T postgres psql -U atlas -d atlas -c "SELECT status, count(*) FROM job_queue GROUP BY status;"
docker compose exec -T postgres psql -U atlas -d atlas -c "SELECT service, count(*), max(last_beat_at) FROM service_heartbeat GROUP BY service;"
docker compose ps; docker inspect -f '{{.State.Health.Status}}' for worker/scheduler/api/postgres
docker compose exec -T api alembic current
docker compose exec -T api alembic downgrade -1
docker compose exec -T postgres psql -U atlas -d atlas -tAc "SELECT to_regclass('public.job_queue'), to_regclass('public.service_heartbeat');"
docker compose exec -T api alembic upgrade head
docker compose exec -T worker python -m worker.health --service worker (exit 1)
docker compose exec -T worker python -m worker.health --service scheduler (exit 1)
Start-Sleep 60; docker inspect Health.Status/FailingStreak for worker+scheduler (unhealthy, streak 5)
docker inspect RestartCount/StartedAt before recovery; docker compose start postgres; re-inspect after 45s
docker compose exec -T postgres psql -U atlas -d atlas -c "SELECT service, count(*) FROM service_heartbeat GROUP BY service;"
docker compose exec -T postgres psql -U atlas -d atlas -tAc "SELECT count(*) FROM app_meta WHERE key='scaffold_initialized' AND value='true';"
pwsh -NoProfile -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File scripts\stack-down.ps1 (final); docker compose ps
end_to_end_steps_run: Read the contract, feature ledger, prior evaluation, progress tail, compose, migration, and all worker source before grading.
Performed the startup ritual and confirmed a clean tree at commit 219760b.
Confirmed the compose topology is exactly postgres/api/worker/scheduler with worker+scheduler healthchecks and depends_on postgres service_healthy.
Reviewed the diff for scope: no React frontend, no F003 domain model, no new runtime/broker/managed-cloud dependency, worker pins are a subset of pyproject, and no unsupported free/Z0 claim.
Ran scripts/check.ps1 -NodeAudit and scripts/test.ps1 and recorded 42 passed / 2 skipped, ALL CHECKS PASSED.
Brought the stack up from a fresh volume, ran stack-smoke, and independently corroborated job_queue done count and fresh worker+scheduler heartbeats via psql.
Verified all four containers healthy via docker compose ps and docker inspect.
Exercised the migration downgrade/upgrade roundtrip and confirmed both tables drop and recreate.

Stopped PostgreSQL, confirmed both health commands exit non-zero with credential-free output, containers went unhealthy (streak 5) with leak-free logs, then restarted PostgreSQL and confirmed both recover to healthy with RestartCount 0 (loops survived without container restart).
Ran stack-down (keep volume), stack-up, and stack-smoke again; confirmed pass, exactly one heartbeat row per service, and app_meta persisted.
Left the stack down; docker compose ps shows no services.
notes: Evaluation performed against HEAD commit 219760b8bd6c2cbcce73c314a3f3bd34b200ed9d on branch stsyg-glowing-waffle (open PR #6 into main).

This verdict covers ONLY the F002 slice-2 worker + scheduler increment (a PostgreSQL-backed job queue wired into the existing compose/DB/migration/scripts pipeline). F002 is an epic and MUST remain passes=false: the React frontend and the F003 catalogue/offer/evidence/Z0 domain model are still out of scope and require their own increments and Level 2 evaluations.
job_queue and service_heartbeat are queue/heartbeat infrastructure tables, not the F003 domain model.
The atlas/atlas strings are documented non-secret local-development defaults; the health CLI and DB-down failures leaked no connection string or credential.
The evaluator wrote only agent-state/evaluation.json and agent-state/progress.md; no product/source/config files, dependencies, or services were modified, and the PR was not merged.
A named local volume (stsyg-glowing-waffle_atlas_pgdata) remains from the restart-persistence test; the final state is stack down (no running containers).
recommendations: Proceed with owner review of PR #6 for the F002 slice-2 worker+scheduler increment; the increment meets its Level 2 contract.
Keep F002 passes=false until the remaining slices (React frontend and the F003 domain model) land and pass their own independent Level 2 evaluations.
Before the next slice, obtain the owner checkpoint required by the contract for the React frontend scope.
